items.rationale: SkillSpector findings show env-variable harvesting and external transmission patterns across multiple tool files; these are code-flow leads, not confirmed absence of input validation, so capped at unknown/medium.
Same findings tagged AFR-09 show tools reading env vars and calling external services/search/browser APIs; indicates a mechanism to vet, not a confirmed vetting failure, so capped at unknown/medium.

osv-scanner dependency-scan findings confirm multiple vulnerable dependencies (aiohttp, idna, chromadb, litellm, pyjwt, python-dotenv, uv) with CVEs, including critical severity, directly evidencing a failure to keep the stack scanned/patched.

Env-variable harvesting findings suggest secrets are read from environment at runtime across many tools, but this is a code-flow lead only, not confirmed evidence of standing production secrets misuse; capped at unknown/medium per rule 3.

items.plain_explanation: Several tool files read secret values (like API keys) from environment variables at runtime, for example in browser_tools.py:17, search_tools.py:27, and main.py:17. That's a normal way to handle secrets, but the scan only shows that these reads happen — it doesn't show whether the secrets are being misused or exposed. We can't yet say whether standing production secrets are a real problem here.
items.fix: Have someone check where these env variables get their values in production and confirm they're pulled from a proper secrets manager rather than left sitting in config or logs. If that's already the case, this item can be closed as fine.

items.plain_explanation: The same set of files (e.g. crews/instagram_post/tools/browser_tools.py:17, crews/markdown_validator/src/markdown_validator/main.py:13-14) read environment values and, in one case, send data externally. This suggests these tools might process input without fully checking it first, but the scan only traces the pattern — it doesn't prove missing validation.
items.fix: Ask the team to review these specific files and confirm that any data coming from outside the app is checked before use. Note the finding at main.py:13-14 for anything that sends data out, since that spot deserves a closer look.

items.plain_explanation: The same tool files call out to external services like search and browser APIs while also reading environment secrets, for example crews/prep-for-a-meeting/tools/ExaSearchTool.py:58. This points to a mechanism that should be reviewed for vetting (checking a service is safe before connecting to it), not proof that vetting is missing.
items.fix: List the external services these tools connect to and confirm each one was reviewed before being added. If there's no record of that review, do a quick check now and document it.

items.plain_explanation: A dependency scan found several outdated libraries in crews/game-builder-crew/uv.lock with known security vulnerabilities: aiohttp (line 19), idna (line 98), chromadb (line 311), litellm (line 399), pyjwt (line 409), python-dotenv (line 410), and uv (line 416). Some of these have multiple advisories, including high-severity ones, so this is a confirmed gap, not a guess.
items.fix: Update these packages to their latest patched versions, starting with aiohttp, litellm, and pyjwt since they have the most advisories. Re-run the dependency scan afterward to confirm the vulnerabilities are cleared.
